author: @nikolaasgerard2454
text: I'm curious what you will use as sprocket on the bottom bracket. On the bikes i know the rear sprockets have less teeth/a smaller diameter than the ones in front. And you're using a small rear wheel. Very curious how you'll solve this, to end with a decent gear ratio.

replies.author: @nikolaasgerard2454
replies.text: A motor that spins faster than a human can pedal?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am just going to do what they do in kick scooters mate

author: @MarioAbbruscato
text: ingenious

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @stuffoflardohfortheloveof
text: I really like that drive wheel system Rob. I’ve never used resin but really fancy trying it out….is it expensive? Easily sourced?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's not cheap mate - but you don't really use a lot - I get mine from the net - actually east coast fibreglass supplies and I find them good but it can be got from a auto repair store - a bit more expensive is all

replies.author: @stuffoflardohfortheloveof
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering 👍

author: @krzysztofniedoba1905
text: Dear Mr. Murray-Smith, could you please break one stick for us?  :)  I'm curious about the mode of failure upon fast impact, especially if it doesn't leave sharp ends, like normal wood would...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: will do mate

author: @Xayuap
text: wow, you could unbolt the rays, hahaha

replies.author: @Xayuap
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I mean, the spokes. I would have unbolted them. Hehe, you happily cut'em

replies.author: @nikolaasgerard2454
replies.text: @Xayuap yeah. Why destroy something you could use in a future bike repair?

author: @reypolice5231
text:
Really enjoyed the drive system. 

The seat looks really high up. Can you explain why?

Maybe it will make more sense to me when it's finished and you are riding it.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I haven't fitted it to me yet mate - I will probably saw a bit off the top when I get the wheels on and sit on it - it's just overlong so I can fit it

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Very cool, just one question, why is the seat so high?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to give me a chance to fit it right when the wheels are on - I will saw a bit off to find a comfortable position fo rme

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering makes a lot of sense

author: @YeshuaGod22
text: Awesome drivetrain design! That seat post though... I mean, once the back wheel's on, what height will it reach?!?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: dunno - but it is that long just so I have a chance to fit it to me once the wheels are on - better too long than too short - much easier to chop a bit off than add a bit on lol

author: @deslomeslager
text: I think you will need 6 washers as well to create some space. The belt will otherwise rub against the tire.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it has nuts as spacers

author: @colouroboros9993
text: are you going to add the paper battery to make a paper e-bike?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no

author: @scotttovey
text: Do you have to widen the rear spokes for the wheel to fit properly?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes mate - it's why the rear stays are parallel

author: @pulesjet
text: Here in the USA the thing must have peddles .   Otherwise it's a EV.  and needs licensed.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: then if you build one stick pedals on - it's why i left the rear cog on the bike wheel  intact - if i ever take this on the road i will fit a pedal and chain

author: @Nick_Tag
text: Yesss I'd been hoping for an alternative bike drive project ever since you mentioned the idea - great to see it coming together!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @En_theo
text: Suggestion : make a tire that does not need inflating by putting silicone instead of air in the chamber. Not sure it will work (never found the time to test it) but I think there is a good chance it would work.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: if you do get round to it let me know how it works out - personally i think it won't work as most silicons are air cure and in a tyre filled with silicon there is no air - so no cure - but i would be fascinated to learn if it works for you

author: @lightcapmath2777
text: WOW! excellent..can't wait to see the next installation. Many thanks DVD:)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

replies.author: @lightcapmath2777
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Respect all that you do and Luke too!! The offer for the coin reserve is still open for him (for the video game console) peace respect DVD:)

author: @angelusmendez5084
text: Btw, do u think this great casein glue would degrade with UV exposure, as plastics do?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am sorry mate they degrade - everything degrades - not even the hills live forever i am afraid

author: @angelusmendez5084
text: Awesome! 👏👏👏

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Thank you! Cheers!

author: @dav1dbone
text: Looking good, you could have the option of exploring a KERS addon.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: for sure

author: @swagswap
text: "Massive Cog" sounds like an insult... or an indie band.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: never thought about it that way - but yes it does lol

replies.author: @ScarletFlames1
replies.text: Now I can't get out of my head that "Massive Attack" is actually a band...

replies.author: @Xayuap
replies.text: I ran to comments just after he said that

replies.author: @swagswap
replies.text: @ScarletFlames1 I was a fan at one time. Haven't heard much about them lately.

author: @karlmyers6518
text: Bloody awesome

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - thanks mate

author: @jasonhaymanonthedrawingboard
text: Truely awesome.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

replies.author: @jasonhaymanonthedrawingboard
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  well if it’s that effective than I guess the will be no stopping you in making a flintstone car or a wacky racer made from paper. Perhaps @Colinfurze could make a version of his screw tank with it and join in. If my memory serves me right Paper mache is fire proof form of micarda. Depending of the on the glue and binders. Paper mache is is like star lite supposedly nuke proof. And resistant to lasers. It seems funny you have made a bike that would be indestructible in an apocalypse. Zombie or otherwise. I say it again truely awesome! The best thing about this is a poor guy with little know how can make it from the leftover of his broadsheet tabloid.

author: @SDS-1
text:
What type of lube would be best for this? Some dry powder ?
I'm only guessing there could be some friction heating with the belts rubbing....

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to be honest mate - i won't use anything - there is just no way this will get to any speed needing that - but if you want to know i guess checking out what suzuki recommend for their belt driven superbike would be a good idea

replies.author: @SDS-1
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering okie Robert.

author: @overunityresearchchannel
text: Want to say thanks for all your work and great content and education you bring us Robert great work man keep  it up 👍💯

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @intelligenttinkering
text: Drive system turned out to be pretty good 👍🏼😌

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's looking good mate

author: @MisterRorschach90
text: Ohh this is so cool. You really could make some pretty awesome designs using paper to make micarta. You could even make tires out of paper and the right resin or binder. If you have a lathe and mill you could make a perfect bike, nuts, bolts, and all.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i think some amazin things could be done

author: @Victor-Meldrew93
text: Love it 👍

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @shay4578
text:
It seems like just using the original gear on the back wheel and a chain would be easier to accomplish.
It's good that you are not doing that because that way more options are explored.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i agree mate
